page: Amazon Web Services is4gen.medium is a storage-optimized virtual machine powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor and custom ARM64 architecture. It features a single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 physical core operating at 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 6.0 GB of system memory, establishing a one-to-six vCPU-to-memory ratio. A key hardware feature is the inclusion of 937 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, providing high-density local storage capacity relative to its single-core compute profile.

Performance benchmarks reflect the single-core constraint of this instance type. The Neoverse-N1 processor delivers an average stress-ng single-core CPU score of 1499.99, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into lower performance tiers, with uncached large-block memory read bandwidth at 19.51 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 9.76 GB/sec. Cryptographic acceleration is supported via hardware AES, though advanced vector and matrix extensions are absent. Network connectivity is provisioned with a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

From a resource density and cost-efficiency perspective, the is4gen.medium offers a specialized tradeoff, pairing a low-compute ARM processor with a substantial 937 GB NVMe SSD. This makes it a highly qualitative choice for storage-heavy workloads that do not require intense CPU processing. Appropriate workloads include lightweight database nodes, distributed file system agents, localized log indexing, and basic storage caching applications where storage capacity and low latency are prioritized over raw compute throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services is4gen.medium is an ARM64 storage-optimized instance powered by a single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 core running at 2.5 GHz. It features 6.0 GB of RAM and a substantial 937 GB local NVMe SSD, managed via the AWS Nitro hypervisor. Network performance is rated at a 2 Gbps baseline. Benchmark results indicate average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 1499.99, but lower multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics, such as a large-block write speed of 9.76 GB/sec. This hardware profile presents a high storage-to-compute ratio, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for storage-dense, low-compute applications. It is best suited for lightweight database hosting, log collection, caching, and distributed storage nodes.

tagline: A storage-dense ARM64 virtual server featuring a high local NVMe capacity paired with a single dedicated processor core.

937 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage
6.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:6 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for lightweight databases, log indexing, and storage caching
